data.title: AB Electrolux GAAP EPS of SEK 0.55, revenue of SEK 2.79B
data.description:
• AB Electrolux (publ) press release (ELUXY): Q1 GAAP EPS of SEK 0.55.
  • Revenue of SEK 2.79B (-9.1% Y/Y).
  • Operating cash flow after investments amounted to SEK 69m.
  • EBITA amounted to SEK 280m, corresponding to a margin of 10.0%. Currency had an impact of –0.8 pp on the EBITA margin.

data.title: Nvidia expands India footprint with $130M lease deal in Bengaluru
data.description:
• U.S. chipmaker Nvidia's (NVDA) Indian subsidiary, NVIDIA Graphics, has leased about 760,000 sq ft of office space in Bengaluru’s Mahadevpura area.
  • The deal is a 10-year agreement valued at over $130M, underscoring its aggressive expansion in India’s AI and engineering ecosystem.
  • The company has also paid a security deposit of over $3.6M as part of the agreement. The lease covers 12 floors at Bagmane Capital’s Memphis South Tower and commenced on April 1, 2026.
  • The transaction ranks among the largest office leasing deals in Bengaluru and more than doubles Nvidia’s (NVDA) known office footprint in India.

data.title: Brixmor Property declares $0.3075 dividend

data.title: Billerud AB Non-GAAP EPS of -SEK 0.69, revenue of SEK 9.82B; gives Q2 outlook
data.description:
• Billerud AB (publ) press release (BLRDY): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of -SEK 0.69.
  • Revenue of SEK 9.82B (-11.5% Y/Y).
  • Sequential sales volume growth of 9%.
  • Adjusted EBITDA SEK 525 million; Adjusted EBITDA margin 5%.
  • The board of directors proposes a dividend of SEK 2.00 (3.50) per share for the year 2025.
  • Outlook for Q2: Benefits from lower pulpwood prices and cost-saving program but challenging market conditions in Europe. Solid market conditions in North America. Broad-based price increases in both regions to mitigate cost inflation for chemicals and logistics. Sequentially higher annual maintenance shutdown costs.

data.title: BP Non-GAAP EPADS of $1.24 beats by $0.31, revenue of $52.26B beats by $6.3B; gives Q2 and reaffirms FY26 outlook
data.description:
• BP p.l.c. press release (BP): Q1 Non-GAAP EPADS of $1.24 beats by $0.31.
  • Revenue of $52.26B (+11.4% Y/Y) beats by $6.3B.
  • Operating cash flow for the first quarter was $2.9 billion, compared with $2.8 billion for the same period in 2025.
  • Capital expenditure in the first quarter was $3.3 billion, compared with $3.6 billion in the same period of 2025.
  • At the end of the first quarter, net debt was $25.3 billion, compared with $22.2 billion at the end of the fourth quarter 2025 and $27.0 billion at the end of the first quarter 2025.
  • "We reiterate our 2026 capital expenditure budget in the range of $13-13.5 billion."
  • 2Q 2026 guidance: Looking ahead, bp expects second quarter 2026 reported upstream production to be lower compared with the first quarter 2026, due to seasonal maintenance predominantly in the Gulf of America and the effects of disruption in the Middle East. The heightened volatility in the oil and gas prices could also impact PSA contracts.

data.title: Elon Musk vs. Sam Altman: AI clash heads to court
data.description:
Technology tycoons Elon Musk and Sam Altman are poised to face off in a high-stakes trial revolving around the alleged betrayal, deceit and unbridled ambition that blurred the bickering billionaires’ once-shared vision for the development of artificial intelligence.

The trial, which started Monday with jury selection, centers on the 2015 birth of ChatGPT maker OpenAI (OPENAI) as a nonprofit startup primarily funded by Musk before evolving into a capitalistic venture now valued at $852 billion.

The trial’s outcome could sway the balance of power in AI - breakthrough technology that is increasingly being feared as a potential job killer and an existential threat to humanity’s survival.

Those perceived risks are among the reasons that Musk, the world’s richest person, cites for filing an August 2024 lawsuit that will now be decided by a jury and U.S. District Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers in Oakland, California.

The civil lawsuit accuses Altman, OpenAI’s CEO, and his top lieutenant, Greg Brockman, of double-crossing Musk by straying from the San Francisco company’s founding mission to be an altruistic steward of a revolutionary technology. The lawsuit alleges they shifted into a moneymaking mode behind his back.

The AP contributed to this report.

data.title: Mission Bancorp declares MISSING dividend
data.description:
• Mission Bancorp (OTCPK:MSBC) declares declares a 5% annual stock dividend..

data.title: Dynatrace rises after Starboard takes stake, pushes for changes
data.description:
• Dynatrace (DT) stock price jumped about 8% on Tuesday pre-market trade after a report that activist hedge fund Starboard Value has taken a significant stake and is pushing for strategic changes.
  • The activist investor is pushing for changes that could help boost the stock, according to a Wall Street Journal report that cites a draft letter. The letter, authored by managing member Peter Feld, is expected to be delivered on Tuesday.
  • Starboard, now one of Dynatrace’s (DT) top five shareholders, is urging the company to accelerate its share buyback program and push margins higher, arguing the stock trades at a discount to software peers.
  • The move comes after Dynatrace’s (DT) shares had fallen more than 17% year‑to‑date, amid concerns over slowing revenue growth and short‑term execution.
